titleStmt.title: Letter from Olive Duffin to Dorothy Duffin, 25 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Olive Duffin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter is part of a rich correspondence between various members of the Duffin family, a large prosperous family living in Strandtown, Belfast. Several family members served in the war, including Major Terence Duffin, who served as a staff officer with 107 Brigade, and later with Royal Irish Rifles; Major Charles G Duffin MC, Royal Field Artillery; and their sisters Emma, Celia and Dorothy who served as Voluntary Aid Detachments (VADS) in Egypt and France, and with the YMCA.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
D2109/9/4/B Nov. 25.1915 7 West Cliff. My dear Auntie Dor, Mamma sent me on a
subscription of £1-1 from all of you for Dorothy
& my collection for the Y. M.C.A. Hut.
It is good of you to send it. We have now got £50
& some more coming. I know it is all in smaller
sums but everyone I wrote to to, with one exception,
sent something. Thank you all very much
also for the Xmas gift also which is 'brave & early'
& very welcome especially to the nursing sisters. I am looking forward anxiouslyto seeing Dorothy
soon. She seems very happy; she refuses to
give any description of Leslie! I had a nice
letter from him, he seems quite ready to adopt
new relations. How will you like a new nephew? I am coming over for Xmas & am looking
forward to it very much. I wish Celia would get
off too. It has been bitterly cold here but to-day there
is sun & it looks better. I put in a good deal of work at the Red X
Equipment Depot, making swabs, bandages etc.
which are sent off to all the various Hospitals.
D2109/9/4/B There is a very nice 'sister' in charge so we know
we are making the right things in the most
approved ways. I wonder have they started
such a thing in Belfast We have had several visitors staying here &
at present Miss Conie Chanley. Many thanks for the Telegraph with Sam's
portrait which I think comes from you.
I am so glad he has got the Croix. I am so sorry for poor Annie Coals. I am
sure she will miss her mother sadly. Is Auntie Cha keeping well? <wqlb Much love & thanks to all.</wqlb <wqlb Your loving niece
Olive. </wqlb
